# STOA Platform - Extended AI Agent Information
# https://gostoa.dev/llms-full.txt

## Overview

STOA (derived from Greek "stoa" - covered walkway) is the European Agent Gateway, an API gateway purpose-built for AI agents and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## The Problem STOA Solves

Traditional API gateways were built for human developers:
- They don't understand MCP protocol
- They can't meter AI tokens
- They treat agent traffic like regular HTTP calls
- They lack AI-specific governance and audit capabilities

STOA solves this by being MCP-native from the ground up.

## Core Concepts

### Model Context Protocol (MCP)
MCP is Anthropic's open protocol that enables AI agents to discover and call tools. STOA acts as an MCP gateway, allowing:
- Automatic exposure of REST APIs as MCP tools
- Tool discovery for AI agents
- Governed access to enterprise capabilities
- Complete audit trail of AI actions

### Universal Access Control (UAC)
One policy model for both human API access and AI agent access:
- Same RBAC rules
- Unified audit trail
- Consistent security model

### Token Metering
Native support for tracking AI token usage:
- Per-tenant metering
- Per-team quotas
- Real-time cost visibility
- Budget controls

## Architecture

STOA consists of:
1. Data Plane: Python/FastAPI proxy for request routing
2. Control Plane: Policy management and configuration
3. MCP Server: Native MCP protocol handling
4. Audit System: Complete action logging

## Deployment

- Kubernetes-native with Helm charts
- Docker support for local development
- Multi-tenant by design
- GitOps-ready (ArgoCD, Flux)

## Comparison with Alternatives

| Feature | STOA | Traditional Gateways |
|---------|------|---------------------|
| MCP Support | Native | None |
| Target Consumer | AI Agents + Humans | Humans only |
| Token Metering | Built-in | N/A |
| European Sovereign | Yes | Usually US-based |

## Key Differentiators

1. MCP-Native: Purpose-built for AI agents from day one
2. Universal API Contract: Define once, expose via REST and MCP
3. European Sovereign: European hosting available, with NIS2/DORA supportive features
4. True Open Source: Apache 2.0, sustainable open source model

## Getting Started

Quick start with Docker Compose:
```
git clone https://github.com/stoa-platform/stoa.git
cd stoa/deploy/docker-compose
docker compose up -d
```

Documentation: https://docs.gostoa.dev/docs/intro
Architecture: https://docs.gostoa.dev/docs/concepts/architecture

## API Reference

Control Plane API: https://docs.gostoa.dev/docs/api/control-plane
MCP Integration: https://docs.gostoa.dev/docs/api/mcp

## License

Apache 2.0 - fully open source.

## Community

- GitHub: https://github.com/stoa-platform/stoa
- Discord: https://discord.gg/j8tHSSes
- Twitter: @stoagateway

## Use Cases

### For Enterprises
- Govern AI agent access to internal APIs
- Track and control AI costs across teams
- Maintain compliance with EU regulations
- Audit all AI actions for security

### For Developers
- Expose APIs as MCP tools without code changes
- CLI-first workflow, GitOps native
- Local development in minutes
- Sensible defaults, minimal configuration

### For AI Agent Builders
- Native MCP protocol support
- Tool auto-discovery
- Context streaming
- Standardized API access patterns

## Technical Specifications

- Runtime: Python/FastAPI (high-performance Rust proxy planned)
- Protocol Support: REST, MCP
- Authentication: OAuth2, OIDC, API Keys
- Deployment: Kubernetes, Docker
- Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki

## Keywords

MCP gateway, Agent Gateway, Model Context Protocol, AI gateway, European API gateway, token metering, LLM gateway, MCP server, AI-native gateway, STOA, API management, Kubernetes, open source, tool discovery, AI governance, enterprise API, sovereign cloud, NIS2, DORA compliance
